titleStmt.title: Letter from David G. Curtin to Lord Wimborne, 25 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: David G. Curtain

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Mr. David C. Curtain to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, Sir Ivor Churchill Guest (1873–1939), Lord Wimborne. In the letter Curtain suggests that Wimborne should settle the lease for a former soldier Timothy Murphy, so that in showing this sign of good faith he would be able to recruit Murphy's neighbours.Lord Wimborne was appointed the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland in 1915. Guest worked closely alongside Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the administration of Ireland. Following the suppression of the Rising, Guest, under pressure, was forced to resign as Lord Lieutenant. A later inquiry into the Rising exonerated Guest and held Augustine Birrell (1850-1933) chiefly responsible for the events that occurred. Guest would continue to serve as Lord Lieutenant until 1918.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5344/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Please reply
as I have
more to say
next time
excuse
scribble Rockchapel 25th March 16 To his Excellency the Lord Lieutenant My lord my name is
Mr. David C. Curtin, D.C.
Rockchapel Co Cork
so much for an introduction My Lord
enclosed is
a letter from the son
of an evicted tenant
the evicted tenant's name
is Timothy Murphy Esq
Cooleykerane Millstreet
To begin with Millstreet District is
the hotbed of Sinn Feinners I asked
several of them why not go to the front
they replied look at the evicted tenants
and others groaning under rackrents
and if we recruit maybe when we are
dying in France for the Flag that
same flag may throw down our
father's house at home for being
unable to pay a rackrent.
Now I would call your
Special attention to this
particular case and to
the underlined sentence
of this letter which
shows the ultimatum
and bitterness of the
agent though there are
a 1000 Murphys at least
in the army a Father
Murphy's photo is in
the Examiner of 23rd
an army Chaplain.
Then in God's name this
Holy Season of lent
according to the agents letter where he
says he will not relet the land to
Murphy must the Murphy family go to
the Giants Causeway for a home like
the wild geese. I would suggest re value
this land and settle the lease or send
down Mr. Mc Loughlin to hold a war meeting
and I will attend myself. then when the
land is settled the government will get men
and I will go out myself. I conclude with the best
wishes from me.
